name: Eternal life (Ankh)
shortDescription: The Ankh is the Egyptian hieroglyphic character that represents eternal life. It is a powerful symbol of life, immortality, and the union of male and female principles.
description: The Ankh, a cruciform symbol with a loop at the top, is one of the most recognizable and potent symbols of ancient Egypt. It represents the concept of eternal life and immortality. The loop is often interpreted as representing the womb or the rising sun, symbolizing birth and rebirth, while the cross symbolizes the union of male and female principles, representing the generative power that sustains life. In Egyptian art and hieroglyphs, deities are frequently depicted holding the Ankh to the noses of mortals, bestowing upon them the 'breath of life' and the promise of existence beyond death. This act represents the divine gift of immortality and the connection between the mortal realm and the afterlife. The Ankh is closely associated with the Egyptian belief in the afterlife and the journey of the soul through the underworld. It appears in numerous funerary texts and is often depicted on sarcophagi and in tombs, signifying the deceased's hope for eternal existence. The Ankh is also linked to the deities Isis and Osiris, whose myth of resurrection embodies the promise of eternal life. Ra, the sun god, is also associated with the Ankh as he represents the daily cycle of death and rebirth. The Ankh's enduring significance lies in its representation of the fundamental human desire for immortality and the Egyptian belief in the cyclical nature of life, death, and renewal. The concept of eternal life embodied by the Ankh is central to understanding Egyptian spirituality and the profound connection between the earthly and divine realms. The Ankh is also seen as a key to unlocking the mysteries of the afterlife, and it is a potent symbol of hope and renewal.

sources.title: The Egyptian Book of the Dead
sources.author: E.A. Wallis Budge (Translator)

sources.title: Myths and Symbols of Ancient Egypt
